name: MCP Google Search Console
what_it_does: Connects Google Search Console to Claude AI, giving SEO professionals conversational access to search analytics, URL indexing status, property management, and sitemap operations across their GSC properties.
use_cases: Identify top queries and landing pages by clicks/impressions with trend analysis over custom periods
Batch-check indexing status and crawl errors for sets of URLs
Submit new sitemaps and monitor their processing status
Compare organic performance across time periods and surface actionable SEO recommendations
Generate comprehensive SEO audit reports combining indexing, performance, and sitemap data
not_for: Non-SEO advertising data (GSC is organic search only, no paid data)
Users without verified GSC property ownership
Real-time ranking tracking (GSC data has a ~2-3 day delay)
best_when: An SEO professional or content team wants to ask ad-hoc questions about organic search performance without manually building GSC reports, especially for multi-property or multi-page analyses.
avoid_when: You need paid search data, real-time rank tracking, or you cannot set up Google Cloud credentials and verify GSC property ownership.

auth.notes: OAuth method uses client_secrets.json from Google Cloud Console for user-level auth. Service account method requires JSON key file and adding the service account email to each GSC property. Both require Search Console API enabled in GCP.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Google Search Console API free; standard GCP quotas apply.
pricing.notes: Open source tool. Google Search Console itself is free. GCP project required but no cost for typical API usage.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error surfaces from GSC API; no custom MCP error wrapping documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read/analytics tools idempotent; sitemap submission and site add/delete are state-changing.
known_agent_gotchas: GSC data has a ~2-3 day delay — agents may report stale data for very recent content
Domain verification must exist in GSC before the service account/OAuth user can access property data
Service account email must be manually added to each GSC property as a user
Visualizations described in docs rely on Claude's interpretation, not native chart rendering

security_score_components.security_notes: Google Search Console MCP. OAuth2 with site owner verification. Site performance data — competitive intelligence. Service account or user OAuth credentials required.
